title: Wearable biofeedback system
abstract: A biofeedback system, that consists essentially of at least one insole and a sensory output device; wherein the at least one insole comprises a sensing unit, and a processing unit; wherein once the at least one insole is inserted into at least one shoe of a person: the sensing unit is configured to monitor a movement of the person; the processing unit is configured to perform a gait analysis of a movement of the person and determine when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; a communication unit of the insole is configured to directly send, using short range wireless communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; and wherein the sensoiy output device is dedicated mainly to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM 1. A biofeedback system, that consists essentially of at least one insole and a sensory output device; wherein the at least one insole comprises a sensing unit, and a processing unit; wherein once the at least one insole is inserted into at least one shoe of a person: the sensing unit is configured to monitor a movement of the person; the processing unit is configured to perform a gait analysis of a movement of the person and determine when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; a communication unit of the insole is configured to directly send, using short range wireless communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; and wherein the sensory output device is dedicated mainly to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 2. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the at least one insole is a single insole. 3. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the at least one insole is multiple insoles. 4. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein communication capabilities of the sensory output device are limited to the short range wireless communication. 5. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensory output device is incapable of cellular communication. 6. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the at least one insole is configured to short range communicate with a communication device capable of long range communication. 7. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensory output device is configured to short range communicate with a communication device capable of long range communication. 8. The biofeedback device according to claim 1 wherein the sensory output device consists essentially of a short range communication unit and a sensory output signal generator. 9. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensing unit comprises at least one capacitive proximity sensor. 10. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensing unit comprises at least one pressure sensor. 11. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the at least one insole comprises a bottom interface for interfacing with a sole of a shoe and an upper interface for interfacing with another insole. 12. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the at least one insole comprises a wireless charging unit for wirelessly charging the at least one insole. 13. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback is audible. 14. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback is visual. 15. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback is tactile 16. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback comprises vocal directions. 17. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback comprises music. 18. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the feedback comprises an external cue. 1 . The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensing unit comprises at least one out of an accelerometer, a gyroscope and an inertial measurement unit(IMU). 20. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein one or more of the sensory output device and the at least one insole comprises a man machine interface. 21. The biofeedback system according to claim 20 wherein the man machine interface comprises one or more buttons. 22. The biofeedback system according to claim 20 wherein the man machine interface comprises a gesture identifier. 23. The biofeedback system according to claim 20 wherein the man machine interface comprises a voice command identifier. 24. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensory output device is configured to short range communicate with a communication device 

 capable of long range communication and is configured to output audio under a control of the communication device. 25. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the sensory output device is a Bluetooth headset. 26. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 wherein the biofeedback system is configured independently and regardless of any communication from a third party, to (a) monitor the movement of the person, (b) perform the gait analysis of the movement of the person, (c) directly send, using short range wireless communication the at least indication about the feedback, and (d) output the feedback. 27. The biofeedback system according to claim 1 further configured to reconfigure the at least one insole. 28. A biofeedback system, that comprises a first insole, a second insole and a sensory output device; wherein each insole of the first and second insoles comprises a sensing unit, and a processing unit; wherein once the first and second insoles are inserted into first and second shoes of a person: the sensing unit of each insole is configured to monitor a movement of the person; the processing unit of the first insole is configured to perform a first gait analysis of a movement of the person; the processing unit of the second insole is configured to perform a second gait analysis of a movement of the person; the biofeedback system is configured to determine, based on the first gait analysis and on the second gait analysis, when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; wherein a communication unit of at least one of the insoles is configured to directly send, using short range wireless communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; and wherein the sensory output device is dedicated mainly to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 29. A biofeedback system, that comprises a first insole, a second insole and a sensory output device; wherein each insole of the first and second insoles comprises a sensing unit, and a processing unit; wherein once the first and second insoles are inserted into first and second shoes of a person: the sensing unit of the first insole is configured to monitor a movement of the person to provide a first monitoring result; the sensing unit of the second insole is configured to monitor a movement of the person to provide a second monitoring result: the processing unit of the first insole is configured to perform a gait analysis of a movement of the person based on a first monitoring result and based on the second monitoring result ; the biofeedback system is configured to determine, based on the gait analysis, when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; wherein a communication unit of at least one of the insoles is configured to directly send, using short range wireless communication, to the sensor}' output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; and wherein the sensory output device is dedicated mainly to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 30. A biofeedback system, the biofeedback system comprises a first insole, a second insole and a sensory output device; wherein each insole of the first and second insoles comprises a sensing unit, and a processing unit; wherein once the first and second insoles are inserted into first and second shoes of a person: the sensing unit of each insole is configured to monitor a movement of the person; the processing unit of the at least one of the first insole and the second insole is configured to perform a gait analysis of a movement of the person; the biofeedback system is configured to determine, based on the gait analysis, when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; wherein a communication unit of at least one of the insoles is configured to directly send, using short range wireless 

 communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; and wherein the sensory output device is a headset and is configured to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 31. A method for operating a biofeedback system, the method comprises: monitoring by a sensing unit of at least one insole of the biofeedback system, when that least one insole of the biofeedback system is inserted into at least one shoe of a person, a movement of the person; wherein the biofeedback system consists essentially of at the least one insole and a sensory output device; performing, by a processing unit of the at least one insole, a gait analysis of a movement of the person and determining when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; and directly sending, by a communication unit of the at least one insole, using short range wireless communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; wherein the sensory output device is dedicated mainly to output the feedback; and wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 32. A method for operating a biofeedback system, the method comprises: monitoring by sensing units multiple insoles of the biofeedback system, when that multiple insoles of the biofeedback system are inserted into shoes of a person, a movement of the person; wherein the biofeedback system feedback consists essentially of at the least one insole and a sensory output device; performing, by processing units of the multiple insole, a gait analysis of a movement of the person and determining when to provide feedback to the person, wherein the feedback comprises stimulus; and directly sending, by at least one communication unit of the insoles, using short range wireless communication, to the sensory output device, at least an indication about the feedback, when it is determined to send the feedback; wherein the sensory output device is a headset and is configured to output the feedback, wherein the feedback is a human perceivable feedback. 33. The method according to claim 32 wherein the performing, by processing units of the multiple insole, the gait analysis comprises performing a first gait analysis by a first processing unit and performing a second gait analysis by a second processing unit. 34. The method according to claim 32 comprising performing a training session by the biofeedback system.
